thesis.invalidation: 1) Price closes below the 200-DMA at ₹121.07 on any day — this would negate the entire trend-template premise regardless of the stop level. 2) Price fails to reclaim the 55-day breakout level of ₹151.71 within 30 days and instead rolls back below ₹143 on elevated volume, suggesting the consolidation is deteriorating rather than coiling. 3) Broad market (Nifty 50) enters a confirmed distribution phase (3+ days of higher-volume selling in a rolling 5-session window) before MOTHERSON clears ₹151.71, removing the tailwind the strategy depends on. 4) Earnings on 2026-08-13 are held with an open position — exposure must be evaluated and potentially reduced before that date to avoid gap-risk that exceeds the strategy's avg-loss assumption of -7.28%.

thesis.narrative: MOTHERSON is a Minervini trend-template candidate trading at ₹143.37 on 2026-07-07, sitting 18.4% above its 200-DMA (₹121.07) — well inside the strategy's healthy-trend zone. The stock is only 7.7% below its 52-week high of ₹155.25, indicating the primary uptrend is intact with no major distribution. The 55-day breakout level sits at ₹151.71; a sustained move through that level would confirm the next leg of the trend and is the natural target confirmation point before the system's ₹172.04 objective. Today's volume is 1.85x the 20-day average, providing conviction that institutional participation is present in the current price action. The 6-month return of +18.1% confirms the stock has been in a sustained uptrend consistent with the Minervini template. The 1-month return of -0.5% reflects a minor consolidation/pullback, which is the textbook setup: buying near-highs of a trending name after a brief pause rather than chasing a breakout. Risk is defined at ₹132.62 (7.5% below entry), which sits comfortably below the 200-DMA of ₹121.07 — the stop does not threaten the trend definition. The primary risk is the earnings date of 2026-08-13, which falls within the 60-day hold window; the position must be managed actively as that date approaches.

verdicts.reasoning: Thesis survives attack on all material fronts. (1) STOP COHERENCE: Stop at ₹132.62 is 7.5% below entry midpoint (~₹143.37) and sits 11.55 points ABOVE the 200-DMA (₹121.07) — this is intentional and internally consistent; the stop is a mechanical risk rail, not a trend-definition marker. No contradiction. (2) INVALIDATION vs ENTRY ZONE: The 200-DMA invalidation at ₹121.07 is well below both stop and entry — no overlap that would make invalidation trigger inside the trade zone. The rolling-back-below-₹143 condition partially overlaps the entry zone low (₹142.65), but this is a *time-conditional* invalidation (30 days + elevated volume), not a same-day stop trigger — structural distinction is valid. (3) R/R AND EXPECTANCY: Risk per share ~₹10.75; reward to target ~₹28.67; R/R ~2.67:1. With OOS avg win +14.39% vs avg loss -7.28% over 1,738 trades and positive expectancy of +2.63%, the math holds even at a 45.7% hit rate. This is how breakout/trend-following systems work — not a flaw. (4) EARNINGS GAP RISK: Earnings on 2026-08-13 fall inside the 60-day window and the avg-loss floor is -7.28% while worst OOS trade was -7.75% — a gap blow-through is a real tail risk. HOWEVER, the thesis explicitly acknowledges this and mandates pre-earnings position evaluation. The invalidation clause addresses it directly. Risk is flagged, not hidden. (5) EVIDENCE INTEGRITY: All evidence items cite supplied data (price, MAs, volume ratio, OOS backtest) — no unsupported claims. (6) VOLUME SIGNAL: 1.85x 20-day average on signal date is cited as institutional participation, but this could equally be distribution. This is a genuine weakness — volume alone is ambiguous. However, the thesis does not rest solely on volume; it is corroborating evidence within a multi-factor template. Not sufficient to veto. ONLY RESIDUAL CONCERN: The worst OOS trade at -7.75% is very close to the avg-loss of -7.28%, suggesting the stop is consistently hit near max — this implies limited slippage tolerance if the stock gaps through the stop. Manageable given the earnings-management clause. VERDICT: No internal contradictions, no disqualifying disconfirming evidence, earnings risk disclosed and managed. Approve.

MOTHERSON (Samvardhana Motherson International Ltd) is a large-cap NSE-listed auto ancillary stock; GSM/ASM stage reported as 0 — no surveillance restriction. No indicators of banned instrument status. Direction is BUY (long only), single entry, no wash-trade or circular-trading pattern possible with a single directional position. Evidence cites only public technical data (price, volume, DMA levels, 52-week high, returns) and a disclosed OOS backtest — no material non-public information cited or inferred. Volume elevation (1.85x) reflects observed market activity, not a spoofing or layering pattern. Earnings date 2026-08-13 is 37 days out and falls within the 60-day hold; critically, the thesis explicitly flags this in both the invalidation rules and evidence, mandating pre-earnings position review — this satisfies the no-trade-window-around-results requirement via active risk management rather than avoidance. Stop (₹132.62) and target (₹172.04) are coherent with stated strategy OOS parameters. No regulatory red flags identified.

Coherence check passes. MOTHERSON is auto parts / global supply chain — sector distinct from current portfolio (real estate: LODHA, DLF; NBFC: CHOLAFIN; pharma: ZYDUSLIFE; consumer discretionary: TITAN). No meaningful correlation cluster concern. Thesis is internally consistent: entry in shallow pullback (-0.5% 1M) within a primary uptrend (+18.1% 6M, +18.4% above 200-DMA), volume confirmation (1.85x avg), defined risk at 132.62, and a clear invalidation framework. The earnings gate (2026-08-13, 37 days) is explicitly flagged in both invalidation criteria and narrative — the thesis is not ignoring the binary event, it is pre-committing to active management before it. Regime caveat (56% above 200-DMA, mixed breadth) is a headwind worth respecting, but MOTHERSON's own trend metrics are strong enough to clear the 'marginal thesis' bar — this is not a marginal setup. The reasoning is sound and stake-worthy. One condition: position must be reviewed no later than 2026-08-06 (one week before earnings) with explicit decision to reduce, hedge, or exit before 2026-08-13 open.

thesis.invalidation: 1) Price closes back below the 200-DMA at 910.62 on any day — structural bull thesis collapses. 2) Price fails to break and hold above the 55-day breakout level (1066.8) within the hold window and begins rolling over below 1014.4 — momentum thesis exhausted without follow-through. 3) Earnings on 2026-07-31 produce a gap-down open below the stop level (963.68) — accept the gap loss and exit; do not average down. 4) Volume expansion dries up (volume_vs_20d_avg drops materially below 1.0) before price reaches 1066.8 — distribution signal, exit at market.
evidence: Close 1014.4 (2026-07-08) is 11.4% above 200-DMA of 910.62, confirming active uptrend structure.
ret_1m_pct: +13.7% (2026-07-08) shows strong near-term momentum consistent with dual-MA alignment signal.

thesis.narrative: SHRIRAMFIN is a financial services name sitting 11.4% above its 200-DMA (910.62) and recovering off a 6-month base (ret_6m_pct: +1.7%) with a strong recent momentum burst (+13.7% last month). The dual-MA trend-alignment strategy seeks entries where short-term momentum aligns with a longer-term uptrend — both conditions are met. The 55-day breakout level at 1066.8 lies above current price (1014.4), meaning the trade is positioned as a pre-breakout accumulation ahead of that resistance, with the 200-DMA providing structural floor support. Stop at 963.68 (-5.0%) sits comfortably above the 200-DMA (910.62), preserving the bullish structure throughout the hold period. The primary risk is the earnings event on 2026-07-31 (23 days away), which falls inside the 30-day max hold window and could introduce gap risk that the stop cannot protect against. Position size of 9 units (pre-risk-sized) limits damage if earnings disappoint. The strategy's OOS expectancy of +1.065% across 3,054 trades with a 41.1% hit rate indicates the edge is real but driven by large wins (avg win: +9.57%) absorbing frequent losses (avg loss: -4.86%), which is consistent with a trend-following payoff profile — this trade targets the +15.0% level that characterises the winning tail.

verdicts.reasoning: Attacks mounted and survived: (1) STOP vs. INVALIDATION COHERENCE — stop at 963.68 is -5.0% from entry midpoint (~1014.4) and sits 53 points ABOVE the 200-DMA invalidation level (910.62). No contradiction; stop triggers before structural break. (2) INVALIDATION TRIGGER INSIDE ENTRY ZONE — Invalidation #2 references 'rolling over below 1014.4,' which is inside the entry zone (1009.33–1019.47). This is a mild soft-stop that could exit a trade seconds after entry. However, it is framed as a momentum exhaustion signal requiring failure to break 1066.8 AND rolling over below 1014.4 — a conjunctive condition, not a hair-trigger. The harder stop at 963.68 is the exchange-submitted order; 1014.4 is a discretionary watch level. Tension exists but not a fatal contradiction. (3) PRE-BREAKOUT vs. DUAL-MA PREMISE — the narrative explicitly calls this 'pre-breakout accumulation' ahead of 1066.8 resistance. The strategy is gujral_trend_alignment_dual_ma, which requires momentum aligned with trend — not a confirmed breakout. Price at 1014.4 is below the 1066.8 trigger, so the thesis is entering on trend-alignment momentum before the breakout clears. This is consistent with the strategy's design (pre-breakout entries are expected; that's where the OOS 41% hit rate comes from). (4) EARNINGS GAP RISK — acknowledged, quantified, and partially mitigated by pre-sizing to 9 units. Invalidation rule #3 explicitly accepts the gap loss without averaging down. The OOS worst trade is only -5.25%, suggesting the backtest does not include large earnings gap disasters in its tail — this is a real residual risk. However, the position is pre-sized and the desk accepts the known gap risk; this is a monitoring note, not a veto condition. (5) OOS STOP EXIT RATE 52.6% — over half of trades exit at stop, consistent with a 41.1% hit rate. Expectancy math checks: (0.411 × 9.57) + (0.589 × -4.86) = 3.93 - 2.86 = +1.07%. Confirms the supplied +1.065% figure. No fabrication. (6) TARGET COHERENCE — single target at 1166.56 is +14.9% from entry midpoint, consistent with the narrative's '+15.0% winning tail' characterization and avg win of +9.57% (target exits represent 18.5% of winners; most wins are partial/time exits, not full target hits — no contradiction). (7) 6M vs. 1M DIVERGENCE — ret_6m only +1.7% while ret_1m is +13.7% confirms base-building then breakout burst. This is the ideal setup structure for this strategy, not a contradiction. No internal contradictions survive scrutiny, risk rails are intact, and the OOS sample (3,054 trades) is large enough to trust the expectancy. APPROVE.

SHRIRAMFIN (Shriram Finance Ltd) is a large-cap, well-regulated NBFC listed on NSE/BSE with no GSM/ASM flags (surveillance stage: 0). No banned instrument concerns. Direction is a simple directional BUY on own capital — no circular trading, layering, or spoofing patterns present; single entry zone, single stop, single target. Evidence is entirely technical/quantitative (price, MAs, volume ratios, OOS backtest stats) with no reference to material non-public information — all cited data points are observable market data. Earnings on 2026-07-31 fall inside the 30-day hold window and the thesis explicitly acknowledges and addresses this gap risk with a defined response rule ('accept the gap loss and exit; do not average down'), satisfying the no-trade-window concern — the strategy does not target earnings but has a documented plan for the event. Position size of 9 units is modest. Stop at -5.0% from entry is well-defined. No manipulation-adjacent behavior detected. All four compliance vectors (instrument eligibility, manipulation patterns, MNPI, earnings window) clear.

SHRIRAMFIN passes coherence check. Portfolio concentration: 5 open positions (LODHA, CHOLAFIN, ZYDUSLIFE, TITAN, MOTHERSON queued) — SHRIRAMFIN would be 6th. Correlated exposure concern: CHOLAFIN is already a financial services name (NBFC/lending), and SHRIRAMFIN is also an NBFC. This is the primary flag — two NBFC-adjacent names in a 6-slot portfolio creates sector concentration risk. However, SHRIRAMFIN's thesis is independently grounded in price structure (200-DMA, dual-MA alignment, 6-month base), not a sector call, and the position size (9 units, pre-risk-sized) is small. Regime view is 'mixed/selective' with 49% breadth — the thesis explicitly requires the 55-day breakout at 1066.8 as confirmation, meaning this is a pre-breakout accumulation, not a blind breakout chase, which is appropriate for weak-breadth regimes where only the strongest setups deserve capital. The earnings on 2026-07-31 (inside the hold window) is a known, acknowledged risk with a clear rule (accept gap, no averaging). The reasoning is sound: entry below resistance with structural floor well below stop, OOS expectancy validated at scale (3,054 trades), and the invalidation conditions are specific and mechanical. The NBFC overlap is a manageable concentration flag, not a veto condition given position sizing. Approving with the note that if CHOLAFIN or SHRIRAMFIN triggers an exit, the sector slot should not be refilled immediately.

thesis.invalidation: Thesis is dead if: (1) price closes below the 200-DMA at 2399.97 on any day (structural trend break, well below stop at 2894.55 — note this is a secondary structural signal, the hard stop at 2894.55 exits first); (2) price fails to sustain above the 55-day breakout level 3212.1 after first touching it, reversing back below 3046.9 entry on a closing basis, signaling failed breakout; (3) the stock is still open 7 days before earnings (2026-07-22) — exit regardless of P&L to avoid binary earnings risk given the 15-day max hold overlaps the 2026-07-29 earnings date.
evidence: Close 3046.9 on 2026-07-08 is 27.0% above 200-DMA of 2399.97, confirming the stock is in a structurally defined uptrend.
52-week high is 3245.0 (as of 2026-07-08); current price is only 6.1% below that high, indicating price is near the upper range with limited overhead supply.
6-month return of +34.9% (as of 2026-07-08) demonstrates sustained institutional accumulation consistent with the turner_trend_filter_sma strategy premise.
1-month return of +4.0% (as of 2026-07-08) shows near-term momentum has not stalled, reducing mean-reversion risk at entry.
Volume at 1.14x 20-day average on 2026-07-08 provides mild confirmation without signaling a climactic/exhaustion spike.
55-day breakout level at 3212.1 (as of 2026-07-08) is the key overhead level; entry at 3046.9 is 5.4% below it, giving the trade room to build before confronting resistance.
OOS strategy stats (6,987 trades): hit rate 45.2%, expectancy +0.324%, avg win +6.12% vs avg loss -4.46%, worst trade -5.25% — stop at -5.0% is calibrated tightly to the worst historical loss.
Earnings on 2026-07-29 are 21 days away, overlapping the 15-day max hold; position must be monitored for early exit before the binary event.
thesis.narrative: ADANIENT is in a sustained uptrend, trading 27% above its 200-DMA (2399.97) with strong 6-month momentum (+34.9%). The stock sits only 6.1% below its 52-week high of 3245.0, indicating it has largely absorbed prior distribution. The 55-day breakout level at 3212.1 represents the near-term resistance to clear; a sustained move through that level confirms trend continuation. Entry at current close (3046.9) offers a 10% target (3351.59) with a 5% stop (2894.55), yielding a 2:1 reward-to-risk ratio consistent with the strategy's historical profile (avg win 6.12%, avg loss -4.46%). Volume is marginally elevated at 1.14x 20-day average, providing modest confirmation without signaling exhaustion. The strategy's OOS expectancy of +0.324% per trade across 6,987 trades gives statistical credibility. Primary risk: earnings on 2026-07-29 fall within the 15-day max hold window (21 days away), creating binary event risk if the position is still open near expiry.

verdicts.reasoning: Attacks mounted and survived: (1) STOP INCONSISTENCY — stop at 2894.55 is 5.0% below entry at 3046.9, consistent with worst historical loss of -5.25%; no contradiction. (2) INVALIDATION INSIDE ENTRY ZONE — 200-DMA invalidation at 2399.97 is correctly flagged as secondary (hard stop exits first at 2894.55); failed-breakout invalidation trigger at 3046.9 closing basis is below the entry zone low of 3031.67, which creates a narrow but real ambiguity: price could enter at 3031.67 and the failed-breakout reversal clause triggers almost immediately. However, the clause requires price to first touch 3212.1 then reverse below 3046.9, so it cannot fire before the breakout level is reached — contradiction does not hold. (3) EARNINGS OVERLAP — 21 days to earnings vs 15-day max hold is a real risk, but explicitly addressed with a mandatory exit rule 7 days before (exit by 2026-07-15). The 15-day horizon ends 2026-07-23; the forced exit at day 8 (2026-07-16) is before the 2026-07-22 trigger date, so the position clears before binary risk. Rule is internally coherent. (4) LOW HIT RATE (45.2%) — not a flaw; expectancy is positive at +0.324% with avg win/loss ratio of 1.37:1 across 6,987 OOS trades. (5) NEAR 52-WEEK HIGH / LIMITED UPSIDE — 52-week high is 3245.0 but target is 3351.59, which is 3.3% above the prior high. This is a legitimate overhead resistance concern: the target requires a breakout to new 52-week highs, and only 19.3% of OOS trades exit via target. However, the strategy is a breakout system by design; clearing the 55-day level at 3212.1 (itself below the 52-week high) is the intermediate confirmation trigger, and the exit breakdown (38.5% time exits) means most profitable trades don't require hitting full target. Risk is real but within strategy design. (6) VOLUME — 1.14x is mild, not exhaustion. No fatal contradictions found; risk rails hold; earnings exit rule is coherent.

ADANIENT passes all compliance checks. (1) Instrument status: GSM/ASM stage 0 — no surveillance restriction. (2) No manipulation patterns: single directional BUY of 3 shares of own capital; no layering, spoofing, or circular trading indicators. (3) No MNPI concern: all evidence is price/volume/technical data derivable from public market feeds (NSE closing prices, 200-DMA, 52-week high, volume ratios) — no non-public corporate information cited. (4) Earnings no-trade window: thesis explicitly identifies earnings on 2026-07-29 and mandates exit if position is still open 7 days before (≈2026-07-22), which is within the 15-day hold window — the no-trade window protocol is embedded in the invalidation rules. (5) Risk parameters are internally consistent: hard stop at -5.0% (2894.55) is above the 200-DMA structural stop (2399.97); 2:1 R:R is aligned with OOS historical profile. No regulatory or compliance red flags identified.

Coherence check passes. ADANIENT does not duplicate existing exposure — current book holds real estate (LODHA), NBFC (CHOLAFIN, SHRIRAMFIN), pharma (ZYDUSLIFE), consumer (TITAN), and auto-ancillary (MOTHERSON); adding a conglomerate/infrastructure name is additive, not concentrating. Regime is mixed (49% above 200-DMA, VIX 14.7), which warrants caution on marginal breakout theses, but ADANIENT is already 27% above its 200-DMA and only 6.1% below its 52-week high — this is a trend continuation entry, not a speculative breakout into weak breadth, which partially mitigates the regime headwind. The 2:1 R/R (5% stop, 10% target) and calibrated stop against OOS worst-loss (-5.25%) are internally consistent. The earnings overhang (Jul 29) is correctly identified and the invalidation rule (exit 7 days before = exit by ~Jul 22) is explicit and enforceable within the 15-day hold window. Learning agenda is served: this is the sixth live test of turner_trend_filter_sma, adding a large-cap conglomerate data point. One flag: with 6 positions open or queued, portfolio is near capacity in a mixed regime — position sizing at qty=3 (~₹9,141 notional) is modest and acceptable. Reasoning is sound; I would stake reputation on the process.
